name: first-revenue-analysis
title: Revenue by product category
description: The kit's first guided workflow: which product category generated the most revenue, answered with inspect-before-run discipline and independent validation. Serves as the template for saving your own workflows.
question: Which product category generated the most revenue?

steps.description: Ground the assistant in the real schema before asking anything
steps.prompt: What schemas and tables are available in my Exasol database? For the tables in the STARTER_KIT schema, describe their columns and how they relate to each other.

steps.description: Ask the question with the inspect-before-run instruction
steps.prompt: Which product category generated the most revenue? Show me the SQL you intend to run and explain it before executing.
review_checklist: Which columns define revenue (price x quantity, or an amount column)?

steps.description: The SQL approved during the ask step. Placeholder below - replace with the reviewed query from your session; exact column names follow data/data-dictionary.md.
steps.sql:
-- illustrative; align with the data dictionary before relying on it
SELECT p.PRODUCT_CATEGORY,
       SUM(o.QUANTITY * p.PRICE) AS REVENUE
FROM   STARTER_KIT.ORDERS o
JOIN   STARTER_KIT.PRODUCTS p ON p.PRODUCT_ID = o.PRODUCT_ID
GROUP  BY p.PRODUCT_CATEGORY
ORDER  BY REVENUE DESC

steps.description: Independent reproduction outside the assistant - same SQL, direct database path
Rerun any time with the validate command above; results are comparable run over run because the SQL is fixed and reviewed. Kit 2 adds the audit/run log and semantic definitions on top of this format.

outcome.deliverable: Ranked product categories by revenue, verified through two independent paths
trust_properties: SQL reviewed by a human before execution
Read-only database access for the assistant
Result reproduced outside the assistant with exapump
Workflow captured in this file for rerun
